generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleSteeringController : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Lower-level chassis control unit managing steering adjustments for the autonomous vehicle chassis"
]

instance MotorSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic Speed Controller (Vedder) converting desired motor speed commands into voltage commands for ACC system"
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuation mechanism for ACC system adjustments based on processed digital twin state"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARPerceptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision LiDAR sensor for environmental perception and obstacle detection in autonomous vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RawLiDARPointCloudData
]

instance ESCTelemetrySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Engine Speed Controller telemetry sensor providing real-time motor state data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorTelemetryData
]

instance CameraEnvironmentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Camera sensor providing visual environmental context for vehicle state monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraVisualData
]

// C4: Data Transmissions
instance RawLiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPerceptionSensor
]

instance MotorTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ESCTelemetrySensor
]

instance CameraVisualData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CameraEnvironmentSensor
]

instance ProcessedClusteringData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudClusteringModel
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorSpeedController, VehicleSteeringController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model for clustering and tracking LiDAR point clouds into obstacle tracks"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2ProcessingPipeline
]

instance VehicleStateBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model representing vehicle state dynamics for ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonProcessing
]

instance TelemetryDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured SQL database storing vehicle telemetry, sensor logs, and DT metadata for historical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorTelemetryData, CameraVisualData, ProcessedClusteringData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabaseManagementSystem
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed data formatted for real-time visualization of vehicle state and environmental conditions"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedClusteringData, VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2VisualizationPipeline
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2ProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS 2 middleware for real-time sensor fusion, AI pipelines, and communication between physical and digital components"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudClusteringModel, ROS2VisualizationPipeline
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonProcessing : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson NX embedded AI/ML accelerator handling latency-sensitive ACC computations"
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveCruiseControlActuator, VehicleStateBehaviorModel
]

instance SQLDatabaseManagementSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database infrastructure for structured storage of vehicle telemetry and DT metadata"
    DTDFVocab:enables TelemetryDatabase
]

instance ROS2VisualizationPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS 2 pipeline processing point cloud data into 3D/2D visualizations for user interface"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeVisualizationData
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time streaming and visualization of sensor data (LiDAR, camera, ESC telemetry) via ROS 2 topics for vehicle state tracking"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight, OperationalStateInsight
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloading ACC system computations from NVIDIA Jetson NX to cloud infrastructure for scalable AI model execution"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizedACC, FaultTolerantACC
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Secure remote deployment, updates, and diagnostics of ADAS modules via SQL database-backed APIs"
    DTDFVocab:provides FirmwareUpdateAction, ConfigurationManagementAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of real-time vehicle state (position, speed, environmental conditions) for operator monitoring"
]

instance OperationalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Processed metrics and logs showing system health, sensor accuracy, and environmental conditions"
]

instance PerformanceOptimizedACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adaptive cruise control implementation with optimized computational offloading for real-time performance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FaultTolerantACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "ACC system with cloud-based redundancy ensuring fault tolerance for complex AI model execution"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FirmwareUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Secure remote deployment of ADAS firmware updates via SQL database-backed APIs"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ConfigurationManagementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote management of system configurations and ADAS module settings without physical access"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C17: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiDARPerceptionSensor, CameraEnvironmentSensor, ESCTelemetrySensor,
                 VehicleSteeringController, MotorSpeedController
]

instance DigitalTwinInfrastructureEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ROS2ProcessingPipeline, NVIDIAJetsonProcessing, SQLDatabaseManagementSystem,
                 ROS2VisualizationPipeline
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  **Control Flows from Digital to Physical:**
  - **Command Types:**
    - **Actuation Commands:** Motor/steering servo actuation (e.g., via Actuation FE or ACC Controlling FE).
    - **Data Signal Translation:** Digital commands (e.g., from ACC Controlling FE) translated into physical control signals (e.g., ROS2 → Wi-Fi UDP).
  - **Control Message Formats:**
    - **Data Translation FE:** Standardizes message types (e.g., aligns actuation commands with physical hardware protocols).
    - **ROS2 Network:** Internal communication for command routing (e.g., ACC Controlling FE → Actuation FE).
  - **Validation & Authentication:**
    - **Implicit in Architecture:** No explicit mention of authentication (e.g., TLS, JWT) or command validation (e.g., safety checks).
    - **Feedback Systems:**
      - **Command Execution Confirmation:** Not explicitly documented (assumed implicit in ROS2/UDP acknowledgments).
    - **Conflict Resolution:**
      - **Not in Document:** Mechanisms for resolving digital-physical command conflicts (e.g., priority rules, fallback modes).
  - **Security:** No details on securing control signals (e.g., encryption, access controls).

  **Not in Document:** Transmission frequencies for control commands, explicit feedback loops (e.g., sensor feedback to digital twin), or real-time constraints.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **hardware-imposed clock synchronization with a strict 100 ms accuracy requirement** for time alignment between physical and digital components. This is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications where deviations beyond 100 ms would compromise real-time coordination, particularly for compute-offloading tasks. The system does not specify explicit synchronization protocols (e.g., NTP, PTP) but mandates adherence to standards mandating stricter synchronization than 100 ms, implying a **low-latency, deterministic synchronization framework** is employed to ensure temporal coherence across distributed hardware components (e.g., sensors, actuators, and ROS2 nodes)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)** for defining software components and their relationships, but introduces **deviations from the standard**:
  - **Architecture Pattern**: The system implements **Functional Entities (FEs)** as ROS2 packages, each hosting one or more ROS2 nodes to encapsulate functionality. This aligns with the ISO 23247 RA but extends it by **decomposing the User Interface FE into two subcomponents** (3D Visualization and Control Panel) and **expanding the Physical Twin Entity** to include general automotive functionalities (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) beyond resource-specific tasks (e.g., OME in the standard).
  - **Component Integration**: Components are mapped to hardware entities (e.g., sensors/actuators in the Physical Twin Entity, data management in the Digital Twin Entity) via ROS2’s **distributed middleware**, enabling cross-entity communication. The **entity-based structure** ensures ownership of functionalities is tied to hardware deployment, impacting the engineering process.
  - **Resource Management**: The system leverages **ROS2’s modularity** to dynamically deploy FE instances, but lacks explicit details on resource allocation (e.g., CPU/memory) or orchestration tools (e.g., Kubernetes). The **deviation from ISO 23247** implies a **customized orchestration approach** where the Physical Twin Entity’s scope is broader than the standard’s OME, enabling cross-cutting automotive features.
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: Coordination is implicit through ROS2’s **topic/publisher-subscriber model** and entity-specific interfaces. No explicit mention of higher-level orchestration (e.g., service orchestration frameworks) or fault tolerance mechanisms is provided, but the **100 ms synchronization constraint** ensures deterministic timing for critical operations."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard does not explicitly define **model fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification methods**, or **uncertainty quantification** for Digital Twin models. The **error detection and correction mechanisms** are not addressed in the documents, nor are **quality assurance processes** for models or data. While the standard emphasizes the Digital Twin Entity’s role in representing the OME, it lacks technical specifications for ensuring alignment between physical and digital states, such as error thresholds or iterative refinement protocols.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Network Infrastructure:**
  The Digital Twin system employs a **hybrid network architecture** combining onboard edge computing and centralized Wi-Fi communication:
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - **Intra-Entity (Onboard):** ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for low-latency, real-time communication between software components (e.g., LiDAR processing, actuation FE).
    - **Inter-Entity (Offboard):** Wi-Fi UDP for transmitting processed data (e.g., vehicle state, object tracks) to external servers.
  - **Network Topology:**
    - **Edge Layer:** Onboard components (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano) handle raw sensor processing (LiDAR clustering, motor/steering actuation).
    - **Centralized Backend:** Data is transmitted to external devices via Wi-Fi, with assumed redundancy for critical paths (e.g., redundant Wi-Fi links for LiDAR data).
  - **Bandwidth/Latency Requirements:**
    - **Latency:** At most **100ms** for critical data streams (e.g., LiDAR object tracks) to support autonomous driving functions (implied by R7 in documents).
    - **Bandwidth:** Assumed sufficient for LiDAR data (point clouds, clustering outputs) but not quantified in documents.
  - **Reliability/Redundancy:**
    - Redundant Wi-Fi links are assumed for critical paths (e.g., autonomous control feedback loops) to prevent data loss.
    - No explicit mention of failover mechanisms or network monitoring tools.
  - **Security Protocols:** Not specified in the documents; assumed absent or handled by higher-layer protocols (e.g., ROS2 security extensions not detailed).
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  **Deployment Infrastructure:**
  The Digital Twin system adopts a **hybrid deployment model** combining edge and remote hosting:
  - **Hosting Platforms:**
    - **Edge Computing:** Onboard components (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano) handle real-time sensor processing (LiDAR clustering, motor/steering actuation).
    - **Remote Hosting:** Data storage and visualization occur on external devices (e.g., servers) connected via Wi-Fi, with no specific cloud/on-premise details provided.
  - **Computational/Storage Requirements:**
    - Edge: Low-power devices (e.g., Jetson Orin Nano) for lightweight processing (LiDAR clustering, actuation FE).
    - Remote: External storage for processed data (e.g., vehicle state logs, reports) but no specific capacity (e.g., petabytes/day) or cloud provider (e.g., AWS) mentioned.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
    - Not explicitly detailed; assumed to scale via distributed ROS2 networks or Wi-Fi load balancing (not quantified).
  - **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - Onboard edge deployment for sensor processing.
    - Offboard centralized storage for reports/data visualization.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance:**
    - Onboard components assume high reliability (critical paths use redundant Wi-Fi).
    - No explicit SLA or uptime guarantees specified; assumed critical for autonomous driving applications.
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  **Standardization:**
  - **Relevant Standards**: The ISO 23247 – *Digital Twin framework for manufacturing* standard is mentioned, which defines a development framework for Digital Twins (DTs) in manufacturing. It is organized into four parts:
    1. Overview and general principles
    2. Reference architecture (RA) – Entity-based design with specific entities (e.g., Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, User Entity)
    3. Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements
    4. Information exchange

  - **Key Components of ISO 23247 (Part 2 – Reference Architecture)**:
    - **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**: Any physical entity with observable presence/operation in manufacturing (e.g., equipment, facilities, products, personnel, processes).
    - **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Manages data collection from and control of the OME.
    - **Digital Twin Entity**: Represents the OME digitally.
    - **User Entity**: Provides interfaces for user interaction (e.g., monitoring, control).
    - **Functional Entities (FEs)**: Smaller units handling specific tasks (e.g., actuation, data transformation, user interfacing). Implementation guidelines are not specified, but OPC Unified Architecture is suggested as a possible approach.

  - **Compliance and Interoperability**:
    - While ISO 23247 provides a foundational framework, no additional details on certification requirements, compliance frameworks, or interoperability standards beyond the standard itself are provided in the document.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin System (DTS) must adhere to **cybersecurity and safety protocols** relevant to **vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communications** and **functional safety** in automotive applications. While the provided documents do not explicitly detail cybersecurity protocols, the **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15)** specifies **latency requirements** for V2X services, mandating a **maximum network latency of 100 ms** for real-time data exchange to ensure timely decision-making in autonomous driving scenarios.

For **safety-critical systems**, the DTS must comply with **ISO 23247:2023** (Road vehicles – Safety-related systems – Safety integrity requirements), which outlines **safety integrity levels (SIL)** and **assurance requirements** for safety-critical components. Additionally, the DTS must incorporate **fail-safe mechanisms** and **risk assessment approaches** as defined in **ISO/IEC 30173:2023** (Digital twin systems – Safety and security requirements), ensuring redundancy, fault tolerance, and compliance with **ISO 15622** (Intelligent Transport Systems – Adaptive Cruise Control performance requirements), which indirectly implies adherence to **functional safety standards** for autonomous driving systems.

No explicit mention of **authentication systems, access control mechanisms, or data anonymization** is present in the documents, thus these aspects remain unspecified."
]
